sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, presents with a history of recurrent kidney stones, having had seven or eight in the past, with the most recent one passing approximately three days ago.
She denies current pain and blood in her urine.
Past medical history includes hypertension and diabetes, with a history of taking metformin 500mg daily.
She has been experiencing occasional insomnia, which she has attempted to manage with meditation apps.
She has been experiencing white coat hypertension, with a blood pressure reading of 150/90 in the office, but her at-home readings are typically 130-140/60-70.
She has a history of consuming fast food and sweets, which she finds challenging to avoid.
Past medical history: Angela has a history of hypertension, diabetes, and recurrent kidney stones.
She is currently on norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension and metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
She denies any allergies.
Review of systems: Angela denies any chest pain, abdominal pain, or other symptoms.
Current medications:
Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure was 150/90 in the office, but her at-home readings are typically 130-140/60-70.
Physical exam: The patient's lungs are clear bilaterally, and her heart exam reveals a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The abdomen exam shows no tenderness to palpation.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Angela has a history of recurrent kidney stones, with the most recent one passing, but she continues to experience hypertension and diabetes, and she has occasional insomnia.
Problem list: Kidney stones; hypertension; diabetes; insomnia.
Plan:
I prescribed metformin 500mg daily for diabetes and will continue to monitor her blood sugars.
I will also have her take her blood sugars before every meal to gauge her diet.
For her hypertension, I will continue her on norvasc 2.5mg daily.
I will refer her to urology for further evaluation of her recurrent kidney stones.
I will also refer her to a nutritionist to help her manage her diet and reduce her sodium intake.
I will prescribe melatonin 10mg as needed for her insomnia.
I will see her in three months to follow up.
Follow-up: The patient will return in three months for a follow-up appointment.
